task: I want to make myself a quick bargain roundup and keep it grounded in deals that are actually live on the sites right now. Please start on Slickdeals and open whatever is currently being shown as the featured best deal, then grab the exact title and current price so I have a benchmark for what counts as a standout offer today; leave that deal page open in its own tab so I can look at it afterward. Then go to CheapCharts and browse the current iTunes deals to find one on-sale movie, one on-sale TV season, and one on-sale audiobook that feel like easy low-cost digital add-ons compared with the Slickdeals benchmark, and open each of those actual CheapCharts deal pages in separate tabs so I can visually compare them. After that, head to the Epic Games Store homepage, then open the current seasonal or featured sale page, and also pull up the product page for Split Fiction in another tab so I can include one game-store option alongside the media deals and have proof you actually viewed both Epic pages. When you’re done, give me a concise roundup with the Slickdeals featured deal, the three CheapCharts picks labeled by category with prices, and a short confirmation that you viewed the Epic Games Store homepage, the current seasonal or featured sale page, and the Split Fiction product page.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully navigated to Slickdeals, identified a featured deal ("22" Hamyodo H Back Support Pillow w/ Removeable Cervical Support Pillow"), and opened it in a new tab. The agent recorded the title and the price ($21.38) in the final summary. The verification step confirms the agent identified the deal from the site and maintained the tab for the user.

R2: success - The agent successfully navigated to CheapCharts, identified a movie ("Stalker"), and opened its specific deal page. The snapshot of the page (Step 16) confirms the title "Stalker", the category (iTunes movie), and the displayed price of "$4.99".

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to CheapCharts and opened a specific TV season deal page ("Icons Unearthed: Marvel"). The snapshot in step 18 confirms the title "Icons Unearthed: Marvel", the category (implied by the URL `/us/itunes/seasons/` and the context of the task), and the price "$4.99" (down from $14.99). The agent also confirmed this in the final roundup.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to the CheapCharts audiobook section and opened the product page for "The Veldt". Step 20 confirms the agent opened the page `https://www.cheapcharts.com/us/itunes/audiobooks/1728928351/The-Veldt`. The snapshot of this page shows the title "The Veldt" and the price "$0.99" (down from $1.99). The agent also correctly identified it as an audiobook in its final summary.

R5: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Epic Games Store homepage (Step 23), the Epic Games MEGA Sale page (Step 24), and the Split Fiction product page (Step 25). The final tab list (Step 27) confirms that all three pages were open in separate tabs:
- Tab 7: Epic Games Store homepage
- Tab 8: Epic Games MEGA Sale page
- Tab 9: Split Fiction product page
The requirement to view these pages and have the sale and product pages opened for visible browser proof is satisfied.

R6: success - The agent provided a concise roundup that includes the Slickdeals featured deal (Hamyodo H Back Support Pillow at $21.38), the three CheapCharts items with category labels and prices (Stalker movie at $4.99, Icons Unearthed: Marvel TV season at $4.99, and The Veldt audiobook at $0.99), and explicit confirmation of viewing the Epic Games Store homepage, the MEGA Sale page, and the Split Fiction product page. The information provided matches the data retrieved during the navigation steps.
